calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-05-2018 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-09-2018 Continued To Jul-30-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION .
Off Calendar Mar-09-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Deputy City Attorney, Susan J. Kawala of the San Francisco City Attorney's Office (415-554-3827) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is GRANTED.

Under longstanding California law, minor differences in elevation on sidewalks and parking lots are deemed trivial as a matter of law and thus unable to support a slip-and-fall case. (See, e.g., Fielder v. City of Glendale (1977) 71 Cal.App.3d 719, 724-26.) The upper limit of triviality is three-fourths inch to one and a half inches. (Id.) In our case, plaintiff produced photographs showing the at-issue depression in a municipal parking lot to be less than three-fourths of an inch. Plaintiff's counsel declares in opposition that the depression "was more than one-half inch in depth," but such an unsupported and conclusory lay opinion cannot create a material dispute of fact given the photographic evidence. In any event, as noted above, the limit of triviality is not one-half inch, but three-fourths.

Plaintiff relies on Kasparian v. Avalon BayCommunities, Inc. (2007) 156 Cal.App.4th 11 for the notion that a trial court should not rely on photographs to find a depression trivial. However, in Kasparian the "poor quality" photographs were taken by defendant. (Id.) In our case, the photographs are plaintiff's and are of good quality.
The depression's triviality is dispositive as a matter of law.

Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (L&M302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION .
Off Calendar Feb-22-2018 Continued To Mar-09-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion for summary adjudication is CONTINUED to
March 9, 2018* to consider the late served opposition. Plaintiff shall immediately provide a courtesy copy of his opposition with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/RBU)
